title: How to combine wider teams Brief/Request Form
excerpt: Hi there. Currently have a team of 25 who all use Monday. Within the team, there are 3 sub teams who all have there own individual request forms, asking their own specific questions to gleen information that then creates…

posts.author: IHar81 (IAN HARLOW)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there. Currently have a team of 25 who all use Monday. Within the team, there are 3 sub teams who all have there own individual request forms, asking their own specific questions to gleen information that then creates a job card. We want to combine the three forms into one - so we can have the same link as opposed to three.

Need an initial screen that will ask visitors is your request A, B or C? when they click B, it takes them to Team B’s form, Click A, team A’s etc

Is this possible and if so, how can I go about implementing

Thanks in advance

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Definitely! You’ll just use Conditions on the Workform. Basically you’ll tell it 'IF they choose Team A, then ask them ‘these questions’, if they choose Team B, then ask ‘those questions’ etc

image659×567 15.8 KB

posts.author: ac.monday (Aaron)
posts.cookedHtml: If you need a more complex form, I found that Fillout.com has a great form builder that integrates seamlessly with Monday.com and they have a free tier that is great.

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @IHar81

Are you looking to combine all three forms into a single form that creates items in a single board, or a directory that points at three forms creating items in three different boards?

If what you are looking for is a directory, you can create a page on your website with three buttons, each pointing to a form, and then share the link to that page.

If you are trying to combine all forms into a single form, our monday app Easyform is a form builder that supports conditional questions in a way that makes doing this easy. By placing the directing question on the top of the form, you can set each question to be shown or hidden depending on the answer to the directory questions.

You can read more about our conditional display here in our docs.

If you have any questions, you can reach us at easyformsupport@easyappshq.com or schedule a Zoom call with us here.

posts.author: IHar81 (IAN HARLOW)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey there Nachliel - Really appreciate you taking the time to respond.

We would be keen to pursue the first option, but at odds how to do this? Is there a document that pertains to this in the link you added to your post?

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Do you mean share a single form? if so please checkout this short demo using Easyform.

As mentioned above, you can read more about our conditional display here in our docs.

If you have any questions, you can reach us at easyformsupport@easyappshq.com or schedule a Zoom call with us here.
